title: requests — data retention windows
description: This module owns NO tables. Retention windows for the underlying data live in the modules that OWN the rows: workflow/approvals owns approval_instances retention, access/grants owns access_grants retention. This file documents the WINDOWS this module DEPENDS on so ops can verify the two are consistent + compliance auditors can trace the retention chain.

expectedRetention.description: Approval instances retained 2 years for compliance evidence. SOC 2 requires audit trails on authorization decisions; 2 years matches the typical audit window.

Grants soft-delete at their TTL expiry; hard-delete 90 days later. Preserves the audit trail for expired grants (compliance auditors ask 'was Bob EVER granted access to X?') while eventually cleaning up.

erasureOnRequest.description: GDPR right-to-erasure requests against the requester or approver flow through the OWNING modules — they anonymise their rows.

notes: Because this module owns no rows, `access-requests:cleanup-orphaned` command is NOT a retention command — it's a consistency-repair command that auto-rejects pending requests whose resource was deleted. Retention proper is handled by the OWNING modules' own retention pipelines.

The compliance module's DSAR orchestrator queries approval_instances filtered on `action_key='access.grants.create'` to include access-request activity in a user's data export. Erasure on the requester's user record cascades to the same rows.
